ddls:
CREATE TABLE "signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal */,
  "signal_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal */,
  "interface" TEXT /* Interface associated with the signal */,
  "signal_type" TEXT /* Type of the signal (input/output) */,
  "init_state" TEXT /* Initial state of the signal */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the signal's functionality */,
  "polarity" TEXT /* Polarity of the signal (e.g., active high, active low) */,
  "frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the signal */,
  "phase" REAL /* Phase of the signal */,
  "voltage_level" REAL /* Voltage level of the signal */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* Active status of the signal (active/inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signals_init_state FOREIGN KEY ("init_state") REFERENCES initial_states ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signals_interface FOREIGN KEY ("interface") REFERENCES interfaces ("interface_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signals_signal_type FOREIGN KEY ("signal_type") REFERENCES signal_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interfaces" (
  "interface_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interface */,
  "interface_name" TEXT /* Name of the interface */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the interface (e.g., SPI, I2C) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the interface's purpose and functionality */,
  "max_bandwidth" REAL /* Maximum bandwidth supported by the interface */,
  "data_rate" REAL /* Data rate of the interface */,
  "pin_count" INTEGER /* Number of pins on the interface */,
  "physical_layer" TEXT /* Physical layer of the interface (e.g., differential, single-ended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interface_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal type (e.g., Input, Output) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the signal type */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the signal type */,
  "range" TEXT /* Range of values the signal type can take */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "initial_states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state (e.g., Configurable, Stable) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the state and its significance */,
  "default_init" INTEGER /* Indicates if this is the default initial state for signals of this type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user's account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, tester, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., state change, configuration update) */,
  "previous_state" TEXT /* Previous state of the signal before the action */,
  "current_state" TEXT /* Current state of the signal after the action */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_logs_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_simulations" (
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each simulation result */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal that was simulated */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the simulation */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the simulation was conducted */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used in the simulation */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the simulation */,
  "simulated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who conducted the simulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_simulations_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_simulations_simulated_by FOREIGN KEY ("simulated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being versioned */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the signal configuration */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Details of changes made in this version */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the version was created */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the version */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date the version was approved */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the version (e.g., draft, approved, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_versions_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documentation" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the document */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the documentation file */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the document */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the document was uploaded */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the documentation (e.g., user manual, technical reference) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the document */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* File type of the document (e.g., PDF, DOCX) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documentation_uploaded_by FOREIGN KEY ("uploaded_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_controls" (
  "control_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal they have access to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "valid_until" TEXT /* Expiration date of the access control */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("control_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signals ("signal_id")
)
